item.title: 賴清德會修憲臺獨嗎！？President Lai amend constitution for Taiwanese independence?|#我的學習筆記 #518 @mynotebooks
item.itunes:title: 賴清德會修憲臺獨嗎！？President Lai amend constitution for Taiwanese independence?|#我的學習筆記 #518 @mynotebooks
item.itunes:author: 我的學習筆記
item.itunes:summary:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. A lot of people in Taiwan say. Taiwan is an independent democracy. But it still cannot become a normal country. The reason for this is the Republic of China. Or maybe. It's because the ROC constitution is different from Taiwan's. There is actually an indelible gap. Between the ROC Constitution and Taiwan. And that's why. We rely on the President. That's what they say in presidential candidate debates. The ROC Constitution is a disaster for Taiwan. Although he was still elected. The President of the Republic of China. Is the President of the Republic of China. In Taiwan, no matter if it's a constitutional amendment. or a constitutional amendment. Basically it all points to. Questioning the existence of the Republic of China. It emphasizes their desire for a Taiwanese identity. The Democratic Progressive Party (DPP),. Which was founded on this basis. Their party platform clearly states. Establishment of a sovereign,. Independent and autonomous Republic of Taiwan. This is what Taiwan should have done. Constitutionalization of sovereignty and independence. Although there is a resolution on the future of Taiwan. And in response to public opinion. Temporarily freezing the platform of the Taiwan Independence Party. So Taiwan's international status is normalized. Still subject to the Constitution of the Republic of China. So Taiwan's international status is normalized. So here's the problem! Now we have President Lai in power. Will he push for a constitutional amendment? He'll take this constitution,. Which he thinks is a disaster, and he'll put it in place. What about rewriting it? But to talk about it. We have to start with. From the standpoint of us,. The descendants of Taiwan's independence. So we have to think in terms of the Taiwan faction. That's the best way to deduce what he's thinking. Let's go from Taiwan. We'll start with Taiwan and the Republic of China. Talking about Taiwan's relationship with the Republic of China. Maybe we should talk about the social humanities first. A classic that must be read. The Origin and Dispersal of Imaginary Commonwealth Nationalism. As author Anderson points out. The reason why nations are imagined. It's because even the smallest member of the nation. Never knew most of their countrymen. and met them.

I've even heard of them. But their interconnected imagery. But it lives on in their hearts. The members of the community may never see each other again. Getting to know each other. But they may have similar interests. Or they may recognize themselves as. A part of the same people. Members of the Nation in Their Minds. Mental Imagery of Intimacy Exists. The simplest example. When your Imaginary Community participates in the. Participating in the Olympics and Other Competitions. After winning a medal. You will join the rest of the people who recognize Chinese Taipei. Share the feeling of being of the same nationality. Together we are proud of our people. Last. The Nation is a Commonwealth. It's because, even within each ethnic group. there may be widespread inequality and exploitation. The nation is always imagined as a profound. Equal Comradeship. Final. It's this kind of friendship. For the past two centuries. Driving millions of people. Willing to kill or die for their people. So we know this. Then we can talk about it. Why does Taiwan still have. Can't become a normal country. Are you from Taiwan? I don't know the history of my own people and country. And the confusion of national identity. That's why we have to talk about the. Relationship between Taiwan and the Republic of China. We have to go back to the history. The Republic of China is 1912. Born in China. Japan ruled Taiwan at that time. Entering its 10th year. And the people of Taiwan, from top to bottom. Forceful resistance against colonial rule. It's still going on. Birth of the Republic of China. For colonial Taiwan. There may be a false sense of nationalism. There may be a freedom to establish a republic. The Yearning for Democracy. But it's undeniable. In the latter part of the Japanese era. Taiwan's Baptism of Modernization. Japan's Construction of Taiwan. Gave Taiwan the model of a modernized country. It also contributed to the future economic development of Taiwan. It laid the foundation.

August 15, 1945. Japan announced its surrender. October 25th. Chen Yi, Representative of Commander Chiang. In the Chinese Theater of Operations. Accepting the surrender of Taiwan Governor Ando. Taiwan is temporarily placed under Chinese trusteeship. This is the first time the Taiwanese have seen. The true face of the Chinese people. After the February 28th Incident. Sentiments of some Taiwanese towards their motherland. From Expectation to Despair. They believe that Taiwan has been reduced to a place of. A colony of fugitive regimes. And the brutal repression of the colonial regime. also appeared on the pages of Taiwanese historians. October 1, 1949. The Chinese Communist Party. Established the People's Republic of China. Announcing the demise of the Nationalist Government. Of the Republic of China. Chiang Kai-shek flees all the way to Taiwan. Nationalist troops occupy Taiwan. Chiang Kai-shek makes a statement. The Republic of China moves its capital to Taipei. Chiang Kai-shek's Republic of China. Losing the Continent. Occupation of Taiwan-Pescadores, Retention of the Golden Horse. Dictatorship and High-Pressure Rule. Ended with the death of Chiang Ching-kuo. But the system of the Republic of China. did not stop because of this. With Taiwan's independence. And the efforts of people outside the party to fight for it. ended the high-handed rule of the Chiang era. Then came Lee Teng-hui and Chen Shui-bian. Ma Ying-jeou and Tsai Ing-wen, the four elected presidents. Three Party Changes. Taiwan was also highly democratized. The Republic of China has existed ever since. But the sovereignty of Taiwan. Who should it belong to? It was against Japanese colonial rule. The Taiwanese who ended the high-handed rule. Of Chiang Kai-shek? Or the ROC regime that moved the capital to Taipei? English Translation of Republic of China. It's the "Republic of China". Before 1996. Only the name of the republic. No Republican Facts. The "Party State" of the Kuomintang, to be exact. After 1996, there is the name "China". But there is no such thing as "China". Because the only real territory is Taiwan, Penghu, and Jinma. So that leaves the status of Taiwan undecided. It's still an ongoing debate.

The Republic of China in the international arena. There are now only 12 diplomatic states. Taiwan's international status is undecided. We can't be "Taiwan". To participate in various international organizations. In the name of Taiwan. Formal diplomatic relations with other countries. Though even if we wanted to. We probably wouldn't be able to. August 15, 1945. Japan announced its surrender. Taiwan was temporarily taken over. By the Allied Chinese Theater of Operations. Allied Commander-in-Chief MacArthur said. Before the peace treaty with Japan was signed. Taiwan remained under Allied headquarters. This is the US military. For the first time,. It was publicly stated that Taiwan’s status was undecided. In 1952, the San Francisco contract came into effect. Japan gave up all rights to Taiwan. But Japan did not. Cede Taiwan to the Republic of China. Signing of the Sino-Japanese Peace Treaty. This is also recognized. Japan believes that. Question of Taiwan's belonging. It should be decided by the Allies. October, 1954. U.S. Secretary of State Dulles. In a report to the National Security Council. Japan had never taken Formosa. and Penghu to China. Japan relinquished sovereignty. But left the future sovereignty undecided. So the U.S. was the main victor over Japan. To these former Japanese islands. have unprocessed interests in these former Japanese islands. December 3, 1954. Signed by the Government of the Republic of China. And the Government of the United States of America. International Treaty on the Mutual Defense. Of the United States and China. 1980. Congress passes the Taiwan Relations Act. Replacing the U.S.-China Mutual Defense Treaty. In 2024, the U.S. even passed the. 2024 National Security Supplemental Appropriations Act. Of which $1.9 billion is designated. For the U.S. Department of Defense. For assistance to Taiwan and regional partners. For military support of Taiwan. And strengthening of the United States. Maintaining Peace and Stability in the Indo-Pacific Region. Investing Critical Financial Resources. Recently, there have also been cross-party legislators. Introducing a new bill to support Taiwan.

Will push for the enactment of the Taiwan Security Act. But there are so many external factors. Now let's go back to the Taiwan part! No doubt about it. Taiwan's sovereignty should be vested in the people of Taiwan. Even if the status of Taiwan is not yet determined. The people of Taiwan enjoy the same. The "right to self-determination" under the International Covenants. On Human Rights. Determining Taiwan's Political Status. Of course you think what you think. What others think is another matter. 1996 to present. Taiwan has held several presidential elections. Three times the party has been replaced. This is under the interpretation of the so-called "Taiwan faction". Every presidential election, every vote. It's all about the people of Taiwan. They're practicing and defending. "The Status Quo of "Taiwan Independence. And these "Taiwanese" believe that. that the Constitution of the Republic of China (R.O.C.). Is based on the "One-China Framework". This constitution is important to Taiwan. It will only cause devastating disasters. And it would even make Taiwan. Into a situation of no return. Because it's very possible. Fall into the trap of the CCP's "One China Principle". Turning Taiwan into China's Internal Affairs Issue. Giving the CCP the name "Reunification". The international legal basis for the annexation of Taiwan. The Battle of Yijiangshan Island in 1955. The Battle of August 23rd 1958. Military Exercises around Taiwan in 2022. From Mao Zedong, Deng Xiaoping to the current Xi Jinping. The annexation of Taiwan has always been the goal of the CCP. Xi Jinping has even made the annexation of Taiwan. As a quest for historical relevance. A Small Goal to Rejuvenate the Chinese Nation. In the process. The Taiwanese believe. That the real mountain is the protector of the nation. Not the Constitution of the Republic of China. It's the U.S.-China Mutual Defense Treaty. and later the Taiwan Relations Act. All of the above. It won't change just because a different party is in power. And Lai Ching-Te as a presidential candidate. As a president, he had to consider. So during Lai's administration. Will he push for a constitutional amendment? If possible. Then the answer is yes! Let's talk about Lysanne! Let's take a look at Toko in his youth. This photo is from 1997.

The National Assembly on July 22nd. Lai Ching-teh raised his hand in different characters. Hands up for Taiwan's Independence! Even in 2014. Lai Ching-Te's tenure as mayor of Tainan. During his visit to Shanghai,. He also talked about Taiwan's independence. Said that of course Taiwan independence is the DPP's idea. But in the program. We fully respect the opinion of the people of Taiwan. In Tainan City Council in 2017. Proposed "Pro-China and Love Taiwan. More than just love for Taiwan. It's also a little hand of friendship to China. Understanding through communication. Understanding, Comprehension,. Reconciliation and Peaceful Development. But Tak's goodwill has not been reciprocated. In the face of the Chinese Communist. Party's aggressive stance in recent years. Pro-China and pro-Taiwan have become. Anti-China and pro-Taiwan. Tak doesn't hide himself. He is a political worker for the independence of Taiwan. Also a pragmatic separatist. has repeatedly and publicly declared that. Taiwan is now a sovereign and independent nation. Its name is the Republic of China. It is not affiliated with China. No need to declare Taiwan's independence separately. He also said:. Taiwan’s future is determined by its 23 million people. After announcing his candidacy for president. De's "Resist China, Protect Taiwan" Proposal. Also shifted to "Peaceful Preservation of Taiwan". in anticipation of more support from the people of Taiwan. Brother Te has just started his political career. The mission of Taiwan's independence. From pro-China to pro-Taiwan, anti-China to Taiwan. To the presidential candidate's proposal to protect Taiwan. These ideas seem to have changed. But in the midst of this. Taiwan's position on sovereignty. And independence remains unchanged. It's not necessary to declare. Taiwan's independence as a matter of fact. The future of Taiwan is up to the 23 million people. This is waiting for the right time. That's the end of Tak's position. Now let's talk about it. Will there be a constitutional amendment during his reign? So, during Tak's reign. Will he change the constitution? He definitely wants to! But he won't do it! Because it's not time yet. And again, according to Taiwan's threshold.

For a constitutional amendment. It could be very difficult! The revisionists want to pass it. "Constitutional Amendment, Naming, and Independence. Complete disassociation from China on the constitutional level. Oppose political merger with China. Finally, a referendum was held. Legal Independence of Taiwan. and applying for membership in the United Nations. Such a proposition. Get Democratic Progressive Party, Time Force. Supported by Green Party and part of Chi-Pin Party. Polls also show that. Support for this idea is increasing every year. October 2013. According to the unified independent poll released by TVBS. 64% should maintain the status quo. 24% preferred independence. Only 7% inclined to unification. October 2021. Annual survey on cross-strait relations by United Daily News. Independence as soon as possible accounts for 18%. Maintain the status quo and become independent again 16%. Maintain status quo forever 51%. Status Quo Reunification 6%. 4% to unify as soon as possible. No comment 3%. The state of radicalization between the UDI and the UMNO. And the polls at different times. Actually, there are also differences. But from the 1990s. After the constitutional amendment. And the direct election of the president. The percentage of supporters of Taiwan's independence. Has slowly increased. And it is usually steadily higher than the proportion. Of supporters of unification. Also lower than the percentage supporting status quo. So our Tak. Waiting for an opportunity. The time is ripe for a constitutional amendment. Or a constitutional option. Let 23 million Taiwanese. Determine Taiwan's future. But during Tak's term of office. I don't think the time is ripe for that. Okay! This episode's. Will Lai Ching-Tak amend the constitution? That's all for now. If you like this episode. Please help us to subscribe and share it! If you have the ability to do so. can also join our membership. Or you can press the "Thank you" button! Also, our DC group is now open! You are welcome to come in and chat with us. My Study Notes. See you next time!

ByeBye!

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ris2-IhqoTQ

中華民國或者是說中華民國憲法跟臺灣

實際上有著不可磨滅的鴻溝

我們賴總統

才會在總統候選人辯論裡面說

「中華民國憲法對臺灣是災難」

無論是修憲或是立憲的相關討論

基本上都指向了

對我們中華民國的存在產生質疑

而以此建黨的民進黨

他們的黨綱就明確寫上

「建立主權獨立自主的臺灣共和國」

「臺灣本應就此主權獨立之事實制憲建國」

雖然後續有臺灣前途決議文

暫時凍結了臺獨黨綱

那麼問題就來啦！

如今是我們的賴總統執政

那他會不會推動修憲

把這個他認為是災難的憲法

重新給修正過來呢？

#中華民國 #中國 #賴清德 #歷史 #公民 #社會 #台灣 #臺灣 #🇹🇼 #民進黨 #總統 #憲法 #憲政 #台獨 #臺獨 #台獨黨綱 #修憲 #中共 #互不隸屬

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝4月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.description:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. A lot of people in Taiwan say. Taiwan is an independent democracy. But it still cannot become a normal country. The reason for this is the Republic of China. Or maybe. It's because the ROC constitution is different from Taiwan's. There is actually an indelible gap. Between the ROC Constitution and Taiwan. And that's why. We rely on the President. That's what they say in presidential candidate debates. The ROC Constitution is a disaster for Taiwan. Although he was still elected. The President of the Republic of China. Is the President of the Republic of China. In Taiwan, no matter if it's a constitutional amendment. or a constitutional amendment. Basically it all points to. Questioning the existence of the Republic of China. It emphasizes their desire for a Taiwanese identity. The Democratic Progressive Party (DPP),. Which was founded on this basis. Their party platform clearly states. Establishment of a sovereign,. Independent and autonomous Republic of Taiwan. This is what Taiwan should have done. Constitutionalization of sovereignty and independence. Although there is a resolution on the future of Taiwan. And in response to public opinion. Temporarily freezing the platform of the Taiwan Independence Party. So Taiwan's international status is normalized. Still subject to the Constitution of the Republic of China. So Taiwan's international status is normalized. So here's the problem! Now we have President Lai in power. Will he push for a constitutional amendment? He'll take this constitution,. Which he thinks is a disaster, and he'll put it in place. What about rewriting it? But to talk about it. We have to start with. From the standpoint of us,. The descendants of Taiwan's independence. So we have to think in terms of the Taiwan faction. That's the best way to deduce what he's thinking. Let's go from Taiwan. We'll start with Taiwan and the Republic of China. Talking about Taiwan's relationship with the Republic of China. Maybe we should talk about the social humanities first. A classic that must be read. The Origin and Dispersal of Imaginary Commonwealth Nationalism. As author Anderson points out. The reason why nations are imagined. It's because even the smallest member of the nation. Never knew most of their countrymen. and met them.

I've even heard of them. But their interconnected imagery. But it lives on in their hearts. The members of the community may never see each other again. Getting to know each other. But they may have similar interests. Or they may recognize themselves as. A part of the same people. Members of the Nation in Their Minds. Mental Imagery of Intimacy Exists. The simplest example. When your Imaginary Community participates in the. Participating in the Olympics and Other Competitions. After winning a medal. You will join the rest of the people who recognize Chinese Taipei. Share the feeling of being of the same nationality. Together we are proud of our people. Last. The Nation is a Commonwealth. It's because, even within each ethnic group. there may be widespread inequality and exploitation. The nation is always imagined as a profound. Equal Comradeship. Final. It's this kind of friendship. For the past two centuries. Driving millions of people. Willing to kill or die for their people. So we know this. Then we can talk about it. Why does Taiwan still have. Can't become a normal country. Are you from Taiwan? I don't know the history of my own people and country. And the confusion of national identity. That's why we have to talk about the. Relationship between Taiwan and the Republic of China. We have to go back to the history. The Republic of China is 1912. Born in China. Japan ruled Taiwan at that time. Entering its 10th year. And the people of Taiwan, from top to bottom. Forceful resistance against colonial rule. It's still going on. Birth of the Republic of China. For colonial Taiwan. There may be a false sense of nationalism. There may be a freedom to establish a republic. The Yearning for Democracy. But it's undeniable. In the latter part of the Japanese era. Taiwan's Baptism of Modernization. Japan's Construction of Taiwan. Gave Taiwan the model of a modernized country. It also contributed to the future economic development of Taiwan. It laid the foundation.

August 15, 1945. Japan announced its surrender. October 25th. Chen Yi, Representative of Commander Chiang. In the Chinese Theater of Operations. Accepting the surrender of Taiwan Governor Ando. Taiwan is temporarily placed under Chinese trusteeship. This is the first time the Taiwanese have seen. The true face of the Chinese people. After the February 28th Incident. Sentiments of some Taiwanese towards their motherland. From Expectation to Despair. They believe that Taiwan has been reduced to a place of. A colony of fugitive regimes. And the brutal repression of the colonial regime. also appeared on the pages of Taiwanese historians. October 1, 1949. The Chinese Communist Party. Established the People's Republic of China. Announcing the demise of the Nationalist Government. Of the Republic of China. Chiang Kai-shek flees all the way to Taiwan. Nationalist troops occupy Taiwan. Chiang Kai-shek makes a statement. The Republic of China moves its capital to Taipei. Chiang Kai-shek's Republic of China. Losing the Continent. Occupation of Taiwan-Pescadores, Retention of the Golden Horse. Dictatorship and High-Pressure Rule. Ended with the death of Chiang Ching-kuo. But the system of the Republic of China. did not stop because of this. With Taiwan's independence. And the efforts of people outside the party to fight for it. ended the high-handed rule of the Chiang era. Then came Lee Teng-hui and Chen Shui-bian. Ma Ying-jeou and Tsai Ing-wen, the four elected presidents. Three Party Changes. Taiwan was also highly democratized. The Republic of China has existed ever since. But the sovereignty of Taiwan. Who should it belong to? It was against Japanese colonial rule. The Taiwanese who ended the high-handed rule. Of Chiang Kai-shek? Or the ROC regime that moved the capital to Taipei? English Translation of Republic of China. It's the "Republic of China". Before 1996. Only the name of the republic. No Republican Facts. The "Party State" of the Kuomintang, to be exact. After 1996, there is the name "China". But there is no such thing as "China". Because the only real territory is Taiwan, Penghu, and Jinma. So that leaves the status of Taiwan undecided. It's still an ongoing debate.

The Republic of China in the international arena. There are now only 12 diplomatic states. Taiwan's international status is undecided. We can't be "Taiwan". To participate in various international organizations. In the name of Taiwan. Formal diplomatic relations with other countries. Though even if we wanted to. We probably wouldn't be able to. August 15, 1945. Japan announced its surrender. Taiwan was temporarily taken over. By the Allied Chinese Theater of Operations. Allied Commander-in-Chief MacArthur said. Before the peace treaty with Japan was signed. Taiwan remained under Allied headquarters. This is the US military. For the first time,. It was publicly stated that Taiwan’s status was undecided. In 1952, the San Francisco contract came into effect. Japan gave up all rights to Taiwan. But Japan did not. Cede Taiwan to the Republic of China. Signing of the Sino-Japanese Peace Treaty. This is also recognized. Japan believes that. Question of Taiwan's belonging. It should be decided by the Allies. October, 1954. U.S. Secretary of State Dulles. In a report to the National Security Council. Japan had never taken Formosa. and Penghu to China. Japan relinquished sovereignty. But left the future sovereignty undecided. So the U.S. was the main victor over Japan. To these former Japanese islands. have unprocessed interests in these former Japanese islands. December 3, 1954. Signed by the Government of the Republic of China. And the Government of the United States of America. International Treaty on the Mutual Defense. Of the United States and China. 1980. Congress passes the Taiwan Relations Act. Replacing the U.S.-China Mutual Defense Treaty. In 2024, the U.S. even passed the. 2024 National Security Supplemental Appropriations Act. Of which $1.9 billion is designated. For the U.S. Department of Defense. For assistance to Taiwan and regional partners. For military support of Taiwan. And strengthening of the United States. Maintaining Peace and Stability in the Indo-Pacific Region. Investing Critical Financial Resources. Recently, there have also been cross-party legislators. Introducing a new bill to support Taiwan.

Will push for the enactment of the Taiwan Security Act. But there are so many external factors. Now let's go back to the Taiwan part! No doubt about it. Taiwan's sovereignty should be vested in the people of Taiwan. Even if the status of Taiwan is not yet determined. The people of Taiwan enjoy the same. The "right to self-determination" under the International Covenants. On Human Rights. Determining Taiwan's Political Status. Of course you think what you think. What others think is another matter. 1996 to present. Taiwan has held several presidential elections. Three times the party has been replaced. This is under the interpretation of the so-called "Taiwan faction". Every presidential election, every vote. It's all about the people of Taiwan. They're practicing and defending. "The Status Quo of "Taiwan Independence. And these "Taiwanese" believe that. that the Constitution of the Republic of China (R.O.C.). Is based on the "One-China Framework". This constitution is important to Taiwan. It will only cause devastating disasters. And it would even make Taiwan. Into a situation of no return. Because it's very possible. Fall into the trap of the CCP's "One China Principle". Turning Taiwan into China's Internal Affairs Issue. Giving the CCP the name "Reunification". The international legal basis for the annexation of Taiwan. The Battle of Yijiangshan Island in 1955. The Battle of August 23rd 1958. Military Exercises around Taiwan in 2022. From Mao Zedong, Deng Xiaoping to the current Xi Jinping. The annexation of Taiwan has always been the goal of the CCP. Xi Jinping has even made the annexation of Taiwan. As a quest for historical relevance. A Small Goal to Rejuvenate the Chinese Nation. In the process. The Taiwanese believe. That the real mountain is the protector of the nation. Not the Constitution of the Republic of China. It's the U.S.-China Mutual Defense Treaty. and later the Taiwan Relations Act. All of the above. It won't change just because a different party is in power. And Lai Ching-Te as a presidential candidate. As a president, he had to consider. So during Lai's administration. Will he push for a constitutional amendment? If possible. Then the answer is yes! Let's talk about Lysanne! Let's take a look at Toko in his youth. This photo is from 1997.

The National Assembly on July 22nd. Lai Ching-teh raised his hand in different characters. Hands up for Taiwan's Independence! Even in 2014. Lai Ching-Te's tenure as mayor of Tainan. During his visit to Shanghai,. He also talked about Taiwan's independence. Said that of course Taiwan independence is the DPP's idea. But in the program. We fully respect the opinion of the people of Taiwan. In Tainan City Council in 2017. Proposed "Pro-China and Love Taiwan. More than just love for Taiwan. It's also a little hand of friendship to China. Understanding through communication. Understanding, Comprehension,. Reconciliation and Peaceful Development. But Tak's goodwill has not been reciprocated. In the face of the Chinese Communist. Party's aggressive stance in recent years. Pro-China and pro-Taiwan have become. Anti-China and pro-Taiwan. Tak doesn't hide himself. He is a political worker for the independence of Taiwan. Also a pragmatic separatist. has repeatedly and publicly declared that. Taiwan is now a sovereign and independent nation. Its name is the Republic of China. It is not affiliated with China. No need to declare Taiwan's independence separately. He also said:. Taiwan’s future is determined by its 23 million people. After announcing his candidacy for president. De's "Resist China, Protect Taiwan" Proposal. Also shifted to "Peaceful Preservation of Taiwan". in anticipation of more support from the people of Taiwan. Brother Te has just started his political career. The mission of Taiwan's independence. From pro-China to pro-Taiwan, anti-China to Taiwan. To the presidential candidate's proposal to protect Taiwan. These ideas seem to have changed. But in the midst of this. Taiwan's position on sovereignty. And independence remains unchanged. It's not necessary to declare. Taiwan's independence as a matter of fact. The future of Taiwan is up to the 23 million people. This is waiting for the right time. That's the end of Tak's position. Now let's talk about it. Will there be a constitutional amendment during his reign? So, during Tak's reign. Will he change the constitution? He definitely wants to! But he won't do it! Because it's not time yet. And again, according to Taiwan's threshold.

For a constitutional amendment. It could be very difficult! The revisionists want to pass it. "Constitutional Amendment, Naming, and Independence. Complete disassociation from China on the constitutional level. Oppose political merger with China. Finally, a referendum was held. Legal Independence of Taiwan. and applying for membership in the United Nations. Such a proposition. Get Democratic Progressive Party, Time Force. Supported by Green Party and part of Chi-Pin Party. Polls also show that. Support for this idea is increasing every year. October 2013. According to the unified independent poll released by TVBS. 64% should maintain the status quo. 24% preferred independence. Only 7% inclined to unification. October 2021. Annual survey on cross-strait relations by United Daily News. Independence as soon as possible accounts for 18%. Maintain the status quo and become independent again 16%. Maintain status quo forever 51%. Status Quo Reunification 6%. 4% to unify as soon as possible. No comment 3%. The state of radicalization between the UDI and the UMNO. And the polls at different times. Actually, there are also differences. But from the 1990s. After the constitutional amendment. And the direct election of the president. The percentage of supporters of Taiwan's independence. Has slowly increased. And it is usually steadily higher than the proportion. Of supporters of unification. Also lower than the percentage supporting status quo. So our Tak. Waiting for an opportunity. The time is ripe for a constitutional amendment. Or a constitutional option. Let 23 million Taiwanese. Determine Taiwan's future. But during Tak's term of office. I don't think the time is ripe for that. Okay! This episode's. Will Lai Ching-Tak amend the constitution? That's all for now. If you like this episode. Please help us to subscribe and share it! If you have the ability to do so. can also join our membership. Or you can press the "Thank you" button! Also, our DC group is now open! You are welcome to come in and chat with us. My Study Notes. See you next time!

ByeBye!

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ris2-IhqoTQ

中華民國或者是說中華民國憲法跟臺灣

實際上有著不可磨滅的鴻溝

我們賴總統

才會在總統候選人辯論裡面說

「中華民國憲法對臺灣是災難」

無論是修憲或是立憲的相關討論

基本上都指向了

對我們中華民國的存在產生質疑

而以此建黨的民進黨

他們的黨綱就明確寫上

「建立主權獨立自主的臺灣共和國」

「臺灣本應就此主權獨立之事實制憲建國」

雖然後續有臺灣前途決議文

暫時凍結了臺獨黨綱

那麼問題就來啦！

如今是我們的賴總統執政

那他會不會推動修憲

把這個他認為是災難的憲法

重新給修正過來呢？

#中華民國 #中國 #賴清德 #歷史 #公民 #社會 #台灣 #臺灣 #🇹🇼 #民進黨 #總統 #憲法 #憲政 #台獨 #臺獨 #台獨黨綱 #修憲 #中共 #互不隸屬

📍讓我們繼續活下去 ► http://bit.ly/2tGL3eB

LINE貼圖搜尋「我的學習筆記」或是點下方連結

我的學習筆記2

https://reurl.cc/qkk38y

我的學習筆記3

https://reurl.cc/n77Rn6

我的學習筆記4

https://reurl.cc/mllNq9

🎬更多的學習筆記

YOUTUBE長影片討論 ► https://reurl.cc/gy2ZR

Tiktok上更多短影片 ► https://reurl.cc/bkjjz3

Facebook關注更多消息 ► https://bit.ly/2TFDZXC

Instagram掌握國際大事 ► https://reurl.cc/GyxEp

方格子學術公眾化成果 ► http://bit.ly/32J6mrK

DisCord群組一起聊天抬槓►https://discord.gg/YmSkbSVrUC

小額支付贊助我歐付寶►http://bit.ly/2Z1s1yA

當然還有PayPal►PayPal.Me/wMyNotebook

😉特別感謝4月份支持學習筆記的朋友

Polya Yang、柯誠譽、Rosemary 、阿里不打、Sion Ein、Roots Ko、Wu Pin-Ying、赤炎、懶虎提姆閣、Roy Li、KaHu Lau、葉小雲、李文傑、陳建宏、Louis Tseng、薩麥爾_MLBB、祈翠、Mariposa Bay、語禪、Krib Stek、張輔庭、whiskyhot886 、Chen Chong Lee、Charlie曾、蔡岳峰、Chung-Ming Wang、上善若水、林雋凱

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
item.content:encoded:
Hello! Hello, everyone! I'm a niu ge. A lot of people in Taiwan say. Taiwan is an independent democracy. But it still cannot become a normal country. The reason for this is the Republic of China. Or maybe. It's because the ROC constitution is different from Taiwan's. There is actually an indelible gap. Between the ROC Constitution and Taiwan. And that's why. We rely on the President. That's what they say in presidential candidate debates. The ROC Constitution is a disaster for Taiwan. Although he was still elected. The President of the Republic of China. Is the President of the Republic of China. In Taiwan, no matter if it's a constitutional amendment. or a constitutional amendment. Basically it all points to. Questioning the existence of the Republic of China. It emphasizes their desire for a Taiwanese identity. The Democratic Progressive Party (DPP),. Which was founded on this basis. Their party platform clearly states. Establishment of a sovereign,. Independent and autonomous Republic of Taiwan. This is what Taiwan should have done. Constitutionalization of sovereignty and independence. Although there is a resolution on the future of Taiwan. And in response to public opinion. Temporarily freezing the platform of the Taiwan Independence Party. So Taiwan's international status is normalized. Still subject to the Constitution of the Republic of China. So Taiwan's international status is normalized. So here's the problem! Now we have President Lai in power. Will he push for a constitutional amendment? He'll take this constitution,. Which he thinks is a disaster, and he'll put it in place. What about rewriting it? But to talk about it. We have to start with. From the standpoint of us,. The descendants of Taiwan's independence. So we have to think in terms of the Taiwan faction. That's the best way to deduce what he's thinking. Let's go from Taiwan. We'll start with Taiwan and the Republic of China. Talking about Taiwan's relationship with the Republic of China. Maybe we should talk about the social humanities first. A classic that must be read. The Origin and Dispersal of Imaginary Commonwealth Nationalism. As author Anderson points out. The reason why nations are imagined. It's because even the smallest member of the nation. Never knew most of their countrymen. and met them.

I've even heard of them. But their interconnected imagery. But it lives on in their hearts. The members of the community may never see each other again. Getting to know each other. But they may have similar interests. Or they may recognize themselves as. A part of the same people. Members of the Nation in Their Minds. Mental Imagery of Intimacy Exists. The simplest example. When your Imaginary Community participates in the. Participating in the Olympics and Other Competitions. After winning a medal. You will join the rest of the people who recognize Chinese Taipei. Share the feeling of being of the same nationality. Together we are proud of our people. Last. The Nation is a Commonwealth. It's because, even within each ethnic group. there may be widespread inequality and exploitation. The nation is always imagined as a profound. Equal Comradeship. Final. It's this kind of friendship. For the past two centuries. Driving millions of people. Willing to kill or die for their people. So we know this. Then we can talk about it. Why does Taiwan still have. Can't become a normal country. Are you from Taiwan? I don't know the history of my own people and country. And the confusion of national identity. That's why we have to talk about the. Relationship between Taiwan and the Republic of China. We have to go back to the history. The Republic of China is 1912. Born in China. Japan ruled Taiwan at that time. Entering its 10th year. And the people of Taiwan, from top to bottom. Forceful resistance against colonial rule. It's still going on. Birth of the Republic of China. For colonial Taiwan. There may be a false sense of nationalism. There may be a freedom to establish a republic. The Yearning for Democracy. But it's undeniable. In the latter part of the Japanese era. Taiwan's Baptism of Modernization. Japan's Construction of Taiwan. Gave Taiwan the model of a modernized country. It also contributed to the future economic development of Taiwan. It laid the foundation.

August 15, 1945. Japan announced its surrender. October 25th. Chen Yi, Representative of Commander Chiang. In the Chinese Theater of Operations. Accepting the surrender of Taiwan Governor Ando. Taiwan is temporarily placed under Chinese trusteeship. This is the first time the Taiwanese have seen. The true face of the Chinese people. After the February 28th Incident. Sentiments of some Taiwanese towards their motherland. From Expectation to Despair. They believe that Taiwan has been reduced to a place of. A colony of fugitive regimes. And the brutal repression of the colonial regime. also appeared on the pages of Taiwanese historians. October 1, 1949. The Chinese Communist Party. Established the People's Republic of China. Announcing the demise of the Nationalist Government. Of the Republic of China. Chiang Kai-shek flees all the way to Taiwan. Nationalist troops occupy Taiwan. Chiang Kai-shek makes a statement. The Republic of China moves its capital to Taipei. Chiang Kai-shek's Republic of China. Losing the Continent. Occupation of Taiwan-Pescadores, Retention of the Golden Horse. Dictatorship and High-Pressure Rule. Ended with the death of Chiang Ching-kuo. But the system of the Republic of China. did not stop because of this. With Taiwan's independence. And the efforts of people outside the party to fight for it. ended the high-handed rule of the Chiang era. Then came Lee Teng-hui and Chen Shui-bian. Ma Ying-jeou and Tsai Ing-wen, the four elected presidents. Three Party Changes. Taiwan was also highly democratized. The Republic of China has existed ever since. But the sovereignty of Taiwan. Who should it belong to? It was against Japanese colonial rule. The Taiwanese who ended the high-handed rule. Of Chiang Kai-shek? Or the ROC regime that moved the capital to Taipei? English Translation of Republic of China. It's the "Republic of China". Before 1996. Only the name of the republic. No Republican Facts. The "Party State" of the Kuomintang, to be exact. After 1996, there is the name "China". But there is no such thing as "China". Because the only real territory is Taiwan, Penghu, and Jinma. So that leaves the status of Taiwan undecided. It's still an ongoing debate.

The Republic of China in the international arena. There are now only 12 diplomatic states. Taiwan's international status is undecided. We can't be "Taiwan". To participate in various international organizations. In the name of Taiwan. Formal diplomatic relations with other countries. Though even if we wanted to. We probably wouldn't be able to. August 15, 1945. Japan announced its surrender. Taiwan was temporarily taken over. By the Allied Chinese Theater of Operations. Allied Commander-in-Chief MacArthur said. Before the peace treaty with Japan was signed. Taiwan remained under Allied headquarters. This is the US military. For the first time,. It was publicly stated that Taiwan’s status was undecided. In 1952, the San Francisco contract came into effect. Japan gave up all rights to Taiwan. But Japan did not. Cede Taiwan to the Republic of China. Signing of the Sino-Japanese Peace Treaty. This is also recognized. Japan believes that. Question of Taiwan's belonging. It should be decided by the Allies. October, 1954. U.S. Secretary of State Dulles. In a report to the National Security Council. Japan had never taken Formosa. and Penghu to China. Japan relinquished sovereignty. But left the future sovereignty undecided. So the U.S. was the main victor over Japan. To these former Japanese islands. have unprocessed interests in these former Japanese islands. December 3, 1954. Signed by the Government of the Republic of China. And the Government of the United States of America. International Treaty on the Mutual Defense. Of the United States and China. 1980. Congress passes the Taiwan Relations Act. Replacing the U.S.-China Mutual Defense Treaty. In 2024, the U.S. even passed the. 2024 National Security Supplemental Appropriations Act. Of which $1.9 billion is designated. For the U.S. Department of Defense. For assistance to Taiwan and regional partners. For military support of Taiwan. And strengthening of the United States. Maintaining Peace and Stability in the Indo-Pacific Region. Investing Critical Financial Resources. Recently, there have also been cross-party legislators. Introducing a new bill to support Taiwan.

Will push for the enactment of the Taiwan Security Act. But there are so many external factors. Now let's go back to the Taiwan part! No doubt about it. Taiwan's sovereignty should be vested in the people of Taiwan. Even if the status of Taiwan is not yet determined. The people of Taiwan enjoy the same. The "right to self-determination" under the International Covenants. On Human Rights. Determining Taiwan's Political Status. Of course you think what you think. What others think is another matter. 1996 to present. Taiwan has held several presidential elections. Three times the party has been replaced. This is under the interpretation of the so-called "Taiwan faction". Every presidential election, every vote. It's all about the people of Taiwan. They're practicing and defending. "The Status Quo of "Taiwan Independence. And these "Taiwanese" believe that. that the Constitution of the Republic of China (R.O.C.). Is based on the "One-China Framework". This constitution is important to Taiwan. It will only cause devastating disasters. And it would even make Taiwan. Into a situation of no return. Because it's very possible. Fall into the trap of the CCP's "One China Principle". Turning Taiwan into China's Internal Affairs Issue. Giving the CCP the name "Reunification". The international legal basis for the annexation of Taiwan. The Battle of Yijiangshan Island in 1955. The Battle of August 23rd 1958. Military Exercises around Taiwan in 2022. From Mao Zedong, Deng Xiaoping to the current Xi Jinping. The annexation of Taiwan has always been the goal of the CCP. Xi Jinping has even made the annexation of Taiwan. As a quest for historical relevance. A Small Goal to Rejuvenate the Chinese Nation. In the process. The Taiwanese believe. That the real mountain is the protector of the nation. Not the Constitution of the Republic of China. It's the U.S.-China Mutual Defense Treaty. and later the Taiwan Relations Act. All of the above. It won't change just because a different party is in power. And Lai Ching-Te as a presidential candidate. As a president, he had to consider. So during Lai's administration. Will he push for a constitutional amendment? If possible. Then the answer is yes! Let's talk about Lysanne! Let's take a look at Toko in his youth. This photo is from 1997.

The National Assembly on July 22nd. Lai Ching-teh raised his hand in different characters. Hands up for Taiwan's Independence! Even in 2014. Lai Ching-Te's tenure as mayor of Tainan. During his visit to Shanghai,. He also talked about Taiwan's independence. Said that of course Taiwan independence is the DPP's idea. But in the program. We fully respect the opinion of the people of Taiwan. In Tainan City Council in 2017. Proposed "Pro-China and Love Taiwan. More than just love for Taiwan. It's also a little hand of friendship to China. Understanding through communication. Understanding, Comprehension,. Reconciliation and Peaceful Development. But Tak's goodwill has not been reciprocated. In the face of the Chinese Communist. Party's aggressive stance in recent years. Pro-China and pro-Taiwan have become. Anti-China and pro-Taiwan. Tak doesn't hide himself. He is a political worker for the independence of Taiwan. Also a pragmatic separatist. has repeatedly and publicly declared that. Taiwan is now a sovereign and independent nation. Its name is the Republic of China. It is not affiliated with China. No need to declare Taiwan's independence separately. He also said:. Taiwan’s future is determined by its 23 million people. After announcing his candidacy for president. De's "Resist China, Protect Taiwan" Proposal. Also shifted to "Peaceful Preservation of Taiwan". in anticipation of more support from the people of Taiwan. Brother Te has just started his political career. The mission of Taiwan's independence. From pro-China to pro-Taiwan, anti-China to Taiwan. To the presidential candidate's proposal to protect Taiwan. These ideas seem to have changed. But in the midst of this. Taiwan's position on sovereignty. And independence remains unchanged. It's not necessary to declare. Taiwan's independence as a matter of fact. The future of Taiwan is up to the 23 million people. This is waiting for the right time. That's the end of Tak's position. Now let's talk about it. Will there be a constitutional amendment during his reign? So, during Tak's reign. Will he change the constitution? He definitely wants to! But he won't do it! Because it's not time yet. And again, according to Taiwan's threshold.

For a constitutional amendment. It could be very difficult! The revisionists want to pass it. "Constitutional Amendment, Naming, and Independence. Complete disassociation from China on the constitutional level. Oppose political merger with China. Finally, a referendum was held. Legal Independence of Taiwan. and applying for membership in the United Nations. Such a proposition. Get Democratic Progressive Party, Time Force. Supported by Green Party and part of Chi-Pin Party. Polls also show that. Support for this idea is increasing every year. October 2013. According to the unified independent poll released by TVBS. 64% should maintain the status quo. 24% preferred independence. Only 7% inclined to unification. October 2021. Annual survey on cross-strait relations by United Daily News. Independence as soon as possible accounts for 18%. Maintain the status quo and become independent again 16%. Maintain status quo forever 51%. Status Quo Reunification 6%. 4% to unify as soon as possible. No comment 3%. The state of radicalization between the UDI and the UMNO. And the polls at different times. Actually, there are also differences. But from the 1990s. After the constitutional amendment. And the direct election of the president. The percentage of supporters of Taiwan's independence. Has slowly increased. And it is usually steadily higher than the proportion. Of supporters of unification. Also lower than the percentage supporting status quo. So our Tak. Waiting for an opportunity. The time is ripe for a constitutional amendment. Or a constitutional option. Let 23 million Taiwanese. Determine Taiwan's future. But during Tak's term of office. I don't think the time is ripe for that. Okay! This episode's. Will Lai Ching-Tak amend the constitution? That's all for now. If you like this episode. Please help us to subscribe and share it! If you have the ability to do so. can also join our membership. Or you can press the "Thank you" button! Also, our DC group is now open! You are welcome to come in and chat with us. My Study Notes. See you next time!

ByeBye!

====

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ris2-IhqoTQ

中華民國或者是說中華民國憲法跟臺灣

實際上有著不可磨滅的鴻溝

我們賴總統

才會在總統候選人辯論裡面說

「中華民國憲法對臺灣是災難」

無論是修憲或是立憲的相關討論

基本上都指向了

對我們中華民國的存在產生質疑

而以此建黨的民進黨

他們的黨綱就明確寫上

「建立主權獨立自主的臺灣共和國」

「臺灣本應就此主權獨立之事實制憲建國」

雖然後續有臺灣前途決議文

暫時凍結了臺獨黨綱

那麼問題就來啦！

如今是我們的賴總統執政

那他會不會推動修憲

把這個他認為是災難的憲法

重新給修正過來呢？

#中華民國 #中國 #賴清德 #歷史 #公民 #社會 #台灣 #臺灣 #🇹🇼 #民進黨 #總統 #憲法 #憲政 #台獨 #臺獨 #台獨黨綱 #修憲 #中共 #互不隸屬

📍特別感謝每位默默支持我們的朋友們，你們給的不只是錢財，更是一段友誼 當你像小蝦米一樣對抗大鯨魚，當你的敵人遠比你想像中強大的時候 發現了這個世界，其實有一群未曾相識卻力挺你的朋友，我想，這就是世界上最美好的感覺了
